title: delphinium californicum subsp. californicum
intro: Delphinium californicum subsp. californicum, also known as the California larkspur, is a perennial plant native to the western United States, characterized by its tall spikes of blue or purple flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as a cardiac stimulant, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory. The health benefits of Delphinium californicum subsp. californicum include its potential to reduce anxiety, improve circulation, and alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, although caution should be exercised due to its toxicity and potential for adverse interactions with other medications.
